description.en: Primary tobacco-growing regions in Sri Lanka: **Galewela, Polonnaruwa, Mahiyangana, Ududumbara, Haliela, Buttala**. Virginia tobacco cultivation began in 1937 via the Ceylon Tobacco Company in collaboration with the Department of Agriculture. Almost 100% of tobacco used for cigarette manufacturing in Sri Lanka is cultivated domestically. Domestic tobacco is also used in chewing products and **beedi** (61% of the national tobacco industry by volume, ~2.3 billion sticks). Grown under contract to licensed buyers; shares all solanaceous diseases and pests (aphid, whitefly, TSWV, damping-off, bacterial wilt).

description.quote: In 1937, encouraged by the Government of Ceylon and in collaboration with the Department of Agriculture, the Company launched its first scheme to cultivate cured Virginia Tobacco. The island's key tobacco-growing regions are Galewela, Polonnaruwa, Mahiyangana, Ududumbara, Haliela and Buttala, with tobacco fields in these areas producing high-end tobacco of supreme quality. almost 100% of the tobacco used for cigarette manufacturing cultivated in the country.
